title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Vaginal bacteria turn newborn skin into a beneficial ‘bioreactor’
items.description: A lipid synthesized on skin may promote brain development—and a swabbing with vaginal fluid can provide it to C-section babies, study finds

items.title: South America—the last continent to be settled—was peopled in surprisingly complex way
items.description: Genetically diverse groups arrived in three waves, some potentially carrying genes acquired from long-ago Australasian ancestors

items.title: Whistleblower alleges Finnish startup’s vaunted solid-state battery isn’t what it claims
items.description: Donut Lab’s assertions of lightning-fast charging and high energy storage have led to a criminal complaint

items.title: Watch a spider’s heart race when traffic gets too loud
items.description: Study captures a hidden stress response in roadside orb weavers

items.title: This simple house may help prevent multiple fatal diseases in African children
items.description: Randomized, controlled trial shows the $8800 design can reduce cases of malaria, diarrhea, and respiratory infection

items.title: Early humans turned favored rock sites into toolmaking assembly lines
items.description: Such prescient planning started 50,000 years earlier than thought, study finds

items.title: Cocaine pollution gives salmon wanderlust
items.description: First study of how drugs affect behavior of fish in the wild adds to concerns about chemical exposure

items.title: This disabled parrot has become king by learning to ‘joust’
items.description: Despite missing his entire upper beak, Bruce the kea is winning at life

items.title: Severe malaria may affect children’s cognitive abilities more than a decade later
items.description: Children who had cerebral malaria or severe malaria anemia have lower scores on tests measuring general cognition and math achievement

items.title: After long wait, Trump nominates a CDC director
items.description: White House taps physician and lawyer Erica Schwartz to lead struggling health agency
